sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Celta Vigo are struggling, being winless in their last three matches, though they managed a solid 3-0 win over Rayo Vallecano recently. Osasuna, on the other hand, are unbeaten in their last three games, with notable results including a 3-1 away win against Rayo Vallecano and high-scoring draws, showing attacking intent.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Celta Vigo have had the upper hand, winning both matches (3-2 away and 1-0 at home). These games have been competitive, often with tight margins, suggesting another close contest is likely today.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Celta Vigo, sitting 7th with 33 points, have a balanced attack (29 goals scored) and a strong defense (23 goals conceded, 3rd best in the league). Osasuna, in 9th with 26 points, have scored 26 goals but struggle defensively, conceding 27, which could be exploited by Celta's forwards like Borja Iglesias (8 goals).

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Celta Vigo, Borja Iglesias is in fine form with 8 goals, overperforming his xG, while Marcos Alonso offers defensive stability with a 7.11 rating. Osasuna’s Ante Budimir is a major threat, scoring 10 goals this season (4 in the last 5 matches), making him a key figure in their attack.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Celta Vigo’s home advantage, superior league position, and recent head-to-head dominance, they are favored to win with a 51% probability per Sofascore’s model. Osasuna’s recent form and Budimir’s threat suggest they can score, but Celta’s defensive solidity might edge them to a narrow victory.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Osasuna leads Celta Vigo 1-0 at Estadio Abanca Balaídos. The lone goal came from Ante Budimir in the 35th minute, capitalizing on a rare opportunity for the away side. Celta Vigo has dominated possession and created more chances but failed to convert, while Osasuna has been clinical with their limited opportunities. The home side will feel frustrated after missing a big chance, whereas Osasuna will look to defend their slender lead in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Celta Vigo's attacking intent
halftimeSections.text: Celta Vigo has shown significant attacking intent throughout the first half. They controlled the ball for the majority of the period and consistently entered the final third with purpose. Several players, including Hugo Álvarez, have come close to scoring, with notable efforts on target. However, their inability to finish key chances has left them trailing. The home side will likely push for an equalizer early in the second half, leveraging their superior possession and chance creation.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Osasuna's defensive resilience
halftimeSections.text: Osasuna has adopted a more conservative approach, focusing on defensive solidity while taking advantage of limited attacking moments. Budimir’s goal from a header showcased their efficiency in front of goal despite fewer entries into the penalty area. Their defense has held firm under pressure, winning a higher percentage of aerial duels. They’ve also shown composure in clearing dangerous situations, which will be crucial to maintaining their lead in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Celta Vigo holding the upper hand in terms of attack momentum for most of the period, with frequent spells of sustained pressure, particularly in the early stages and midway through the half. Osasuna had their moments, with a notable shift in momentum leading up to their goal. Towards the end of the half, there were brief exchanges of attacking intent from both sides, but Celta Vigo remained the more aggressive team in pushing forward.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Celta Vigo, Hugo Álvarez stands out as a potential game-changer in the second half, having already tested the Osasuna defense with shots on target. Miguel Román has also been influential with key passes and big chance creation. On the Osasuna side, Ante Budimir remains a constant threat up front, especially with his aerial ability. Raúl Moro, with an assist already, could be pivotal in creating more opportunities for the away team if they counterattack effectively.
